alternative_parents.description: Organic Compounds containing a benzene ring which bears at least one carboxyl group.
alternative_parents.name: Benzoic acids

alternative_parents.description: An arylsulfonic acid carrying the sulfonic acid group at the 1-position, and no substitute at the 2-position of the aromatic ring.
alternative_parents.name: 1-sulfo,2-unsubstituted aromatic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an acyl moiety of benzoic acid with the formula (C6H5CO-).
alternative_parents.name: Benzoyl derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a phenol moiety, which is a benzene bearing a hydroxyl group.
alternative_parents.name: Phenols

alternative_parents.description: N-aliphatic amine derivatives of 1,3,5-triazines. 1,3,5-triazines are aromatic compounds having three nitrogen ring atoms at the 1-, 3-, and 5- positions.
alternative_parents.name: N-aliphatic s-triazines

alternative_parents.description: Fatty acids in which the chain bears a hydroxyl group.
alternative_parents.name: Hydroxy fatty acids

alternative_parents.description: Aromatic compounds containing a 1,3,5-triazine ring that is substituted at the 2-position with a chlorine atom.
alternative_parents.name: Chloro-s-triazines

alternative_parents.description: Fatty acids with a chain that contains at least one CC double bond.
alternative_parents.name: Unsaturated fatty acids

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a sulfonyl group( which as the general structure RS(=O)2R' (R,R' =alkyl, aryl)) attached to two carbon atoms.
alternative_parents.name: Sulfones

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing the sulfonic acid group, which has the general structure RS(=O)2OH (R is not a hydrogen atom).
alternative_parents.name: Organosulfonic acids

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing an aromatic ring where a carbon atom is linked to an hetero atom.
alternative_parents.name: Heteroaromatic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Ionic derivatives of carboxylic acid.
alternative_parents.name: Carboxylic acid salts

alternative_parents.description: Derivatives of diazene(diimide), HN=NH, wherein both hydrogens are substituted by hydrocarbyl groups, e.g. PhN=NPh azobenzene or diphenyldiazene.
alternative_parents.name: Azo compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing metals and halogens. Some are ionic while others are covalently bonded.
alternative_parents.name: Organic metal halides

alternative_parents.description: Carboxylic acids containing exactly one carboxyl groups.
alternative_parents.name: Monocarboxylic acids and derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing the dialkyl ether functional group, with the formula ROR', where R and R' are alkyl groups.
alternative_parents.name: Dialkyl ethers

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a carboxylic acid group with the formula -C(=O)OH.
alternative_parents.name: Carboxylic acids

alternative_parents.description: 1,3-dipolar compounds with the general structure X:-C=Z<-> X+=C-Z- (X = C or N; Z = C, N, or O).
alternative_parents.name: Carbene-type 1,3-dipolar compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an heterocycle with at least one nitrogen atom and one carbon atom linked to each other.
alternative_parents.name: Azacyclic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a bond between carbon a pnictogen atom. Pnictogens are p-block element atoms that are in the group 15 of the periodic table.
alternative_parents.name: Organopnictogen compounds

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a chemical bond between a carbon atom and a chlorine atom.
alternative_parents.name: Organochlorides

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an oxide group.
alternative_parents.name: Organic oxides

alternative_parents.description: Organic salt compounds containing a copper atom in its ionic form.
alternative_parents.name: Organic copper salts

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing an imine functional group, with the general structure RN=CR2 (R = H, hydrocarbyl).
alternative_parents.name: Imines

alternative_parents.description: Derivatives of hydrocarbons obtained by substituting one or more carbon atoms by an heteroatom. They contain at least one carbon atom and heteroatom.
alternative_parents.name: Hydrocarbon derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds that have a negative electric charge.
alternative_parents.name: Organic anions

class.description: Aromatic compounds containing one monocyclic ring system consisting of benzene.
class.name: Benzene and substituted derivatives

description: This compound belongs to the class of organic compounds known as 4-sulfobenzoic acids. These are sulfobenzoic acid carrying the sulfonyl group at the 4-position of the benzene group.

direct_parent.description: Sulfobenzoic acid carrying the sulfonyl group at the 4-position of the benzene group.
direct_parent.name: 4-sulfobenzoic acids

intermediate_nodes.description: Benzoic acids carrying a sulfonyl group on the benzene ring.
intermediate_nodes.name: Sulfobenzoic acids

kingdom.description: Compounds that contain at least one carbon atom, excluding isocyanide/cyanide and their non-hydrocarbyl derivatives, thiophosgene, carbon diselenide, carbon monosulfide, carbon disulfide, carbon subsulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, Carbon suboxide, and dicarbon monoxide.
kingdom.name: Organic compounds

subclass.description: Organic compounds containing a sulfonic acid or a derivative thereof that is linked to a benzene ring.
subclass.name: Benzenesulfonic acids and derivatives

superclass.description: Aromatic compounds containing one or more benzene rings.
superclass.name: Benzenoids
